claim: Claude Code on the web launched October 20, 2025 as a research preview for Pro/Max users at claude.com/code, running tasks on Anthropic-managed cloud infrastructure in isolated sandboxes, with a dedicated Code tab in the iOS app for mobile use.
quote: Claude Code on the web lets you kick off coding sessions without opening your terminal... Cloud-based sessions share rate limits with all other Claude Code usage.

claim: Claude Code Web wraps the CLI in a container running in YOLO mode (--dangerously-skip-permissions), with selectable network isolation modes from no-network to custom allowlists, plus a 'teleport' feature to copy a session to your local CLI.
quote: Operates in 'YOLO mode'—--dangerously-skip-permissions—meaning no per-command approval is needed... Users can select environments ranging from 'No network access' to custom allowlists or unrestricted access ('*').

claim: Anthropic shipped 'Remote Control,' a Claude Code mobile mode for iPhone and Android, initially limited to Claude Max ($100-$200/mo) subscribers with Pro to follow.
quote: Anthropic has added a new mode, Remote Control, that lets users issue commands to Claude Code from their iPhone and Android smartphones — starting with subscribers to Anthropic's Claude Max ($100-$200 USD monthly) subscription tier.

claim: Claude Code mobile is an early preview accessed via a Code tab in the iOS app, letting users create PRs from a phone using the same Anthropic-managed container runtime as the web version.
quote: create pull requests from a phone, which one commenter called 'a dream come true for quick fixes'... mobile Claude Code is available only to 'Max and Pro subscribers'

claim: anthropics/claude-code-action (7,313 stars, 1,765 forks as of April 2026) auto-detects mode from workflow context — @claude mentions in PR/issue comments, issue assignments, or explicit prompts — and runs on the user's own GitHub Actions runner with API calls to Anthropic, Bedrock, Vertex, or Microsoft Foundry.
quote: intelligently detects when to activate based on your workflow context—whether responding to @claude mentions, issue assignments, or executing automation tasks with explicit prompts

claim: anthropics/claude-code, the core CLI repo, has 118,440 stars and 19,694 forks as of April 2026, dwarfing the GitHub Action wrapper and indicating heavy local-terminal adoption.
quote: Claude Code is an agentic coding tool that lives in your terminal, understands your codebase, and helps you code faster

claim: Cursor 1.1 (June 12, 2025) shipped Background Agents in Slack — mention @Cursor in any thread, the agent runs remotely in a secure environment, reads thread context, and opens a GitHub PR.
quote: enables launching AI agents directly from Slack by mentioning @Cursor. Agents can 'read the thread, understand what's going on, and create PRs in GitHub' without switching applications... agents run remotely in a secure environment

claim: Cursor's Slack agent spins up an isolated VM, clones the repo, writes code, runs tests, and notifies Slack with a PR link on completion — fully cloud-runtime, not piggybacking on the user's IDE.
quote: Cursor launches an isolated virtual machine to work with your repo in a remote dev environment, and the agent reads context in the Slack thread, writes code, and provides status updates

claim: OpenAI Codex Slack integration lets users mention @Codex in a channel/thread; Codex selects an environment and repo automatically, runs the task in OpenAI's cloud, and replies with a link to merge or pull the work locally — requires Plus/Pro/Business/Enterprise/Edu plan.
quote: mention @Codex with a prompt, and Codex creates a cloud task and replies with the results... You need a Plus, Pro, Business, Enterprise, or Edu plan

claim: OpenAI Codex went generally available with Slack integration, admin tools, and a pay-as-you-go SDK — positioned as a cloud-first, async, parallel-task agent powered by codex-1 (a coding-tuned o3 variant).
quote: Codex is now generally available with Slack integration, admin tools, and a pay-as-you-go SDK that lets you build the same agent into your own workflows

claim: openai/codex, the open-source terminal coding agent, has 78,266 stars and 11,175 forks as of April 2026 — a parallel community presence to the cloud Codex offering.
quote: Lightweight coding agent that runs in your terminal

claim: Devin 2.0 dropped the entry price from $500/mo to $20/mo with pay-as-you-go at $2.25 per Agent Compute Unit (ACU), with Team at $500/mo for 250 ACUs included.
quote: The Core plan starts at $20/month with pay-as-you-go at $2.25 per ACU, while the Team plan at $500/month includes 250 Agent Compute Units

claim: Slack integration is bundled at the Devin Pro tier ($20/mo) and above; the Free tier excludes it, anchoring Devin as a Slack-native async agent for paying users.
quote: Slack Integration: Available on Pro and higher plans, with 'Slack, Linear, MCP integrations' included

claim: Devin's 2026 release notes call out smoother Slack and Linear integrations to start sessions without context-switching, and now block non-Devin users from posting in Devin Slack threads.
quote: Users without Devin accounts are now blocked from messaging in Devin Slack threads

claim: Heavy Slack/GitHub-issue assignment of Devin tasks tends to push real spend back to $300-500/mo via ACU consumption, despite the headline $20 price.
quote: If your team is assigning Devin tasks through Slack or GitHub issues asynchronously — which is exactly how it's designed to work — you may not know the ACU tab until the invoice arrives

claim: Replit Agent is browser/IDE-first inside the Replit workspace and integrates with Slack, Notion, BigQuery as data sources rather than as command surfaces — it doesn't have a 'message me and I'll ship a PR' Slack mode.
quote: In your Replit Workspace, you can describe an app you want to build, ask a question, research a topic, or pull data from connected services like BigQuery, Slack, or Notion
